title: galium verum subsp. maritimum
intro: Galium verum subsp. maritimum, also known as the sea sweet violet, is a subspecies of the common violets that grows in coastal areas. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. Galium verum subsp. maritimum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for use in traditional medicine.
